study_notes.heading: My Little Pictionary (Key Vocabulary)
points: Rack: A stand used to keep things in an organized manner.
Sack: A large bag used for storing and carrying various goods.
Lazy: A quality of being unwilling to work or exert effort.
Expo: A large-scale international exhibition or trade fair.
Humanoid: A robot or entity that possesses a human-like appearance.

study_notes.heading: The Trick Robot (Prose)
points: Vicky was a lazy boy who refused to do his homework or keep his belongings in their proper places.
Vicky requested his father to buy him a robot to handle all his chores.
Vicky's father bought a 'trick robot' to teach Vicky a lesson and help him become active.
Defiance 1: Refused to make salad because it does not eat salad.
Defiance 2: Refused to clean the room to avoid straining its back.
Defiance 3: Refused to charge Vicky's phone and told him to do it himself.
Defiance 4: Refused to play a song and asked Vicky to play one for it instead.
Outcome: Vicky realized that he could not depend on the robot and decided to do all his work himself. He eventually asked his father to sell the robot to an 'active master'.

study_notes.heading: Grammar: Singular and Plural
points: Singular: Refers to one person or thing.
Plural: Refers to more than one person or thing.
Rule 1 (Basic): Add -s (e.g., Bat -> Bats, Cup -> Cups).
Rule 2 (ch, sh, s, ss, x): Add -es (e.g., Torch -> Torches, Brush -> Brushes, Box -> Boxes).
Rule 3 (y after vowel): Add -s (e.g., Key -> Keys, Toy -> Toys).
Rule 4 (y after consonant): Change 'y' to -ies (e.g., Lady -> Ladies, Baby -> Babies).

study_notes.heading: My Robot (Poem)
points: Physical Description: The robot is big, strong, smart, and tall.
Capabilities: It walks along with a rhyming song and welcomes everyone with a greeting call.
Mechanism: It uses wheels to move front and back and stores sacks in racks.
Work Ethic: It never requires food or rest and always works with great zest (interest).
Rhyming Words: Tall/All, Song/Along, Rack/Sack, Rest/Best.

study_notes.heading: Robot Expo (Supplementary)
points: Humanoid: Welcomed visitors and knew their names.
Scanning Robot: Could name the things people were carrying.
Dancing Robot: Danced to songs played by visitors.
Animal Robots: Included Butterfly robot, ant robot, and fish robot.
Cook Robot: Cooked and served dishes like omelettes.
Main Parts: Controller (Brain), Mechanical Parts (Movement), Sensors (Detection).

questions.question: Vicky wanted a _______ to do his work.
What kind of robot did Vicky's father buy?
True or False: Vicky was an active boy.
Who said 'The robot will do all your work'?
Who said 'I will do all the work myself' at the end?
Which noun is the odd one out: City, boy, book, Chennai?
Which part acts as the 'brain' of the robot?
What does 'zest' mean in the poem?
